title: As the legislature convened Governor Newsom’s special session to tax oil production and increase the cost of gasoline, Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) announced the introduct
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Senate Bill 15 aims to encourage the production of environmentally responsible oil in California.
Much of the crude oil imported into California comes from foreign nations with demonstrated human rights abuses.
Senate Bill 15 would require the Air Resources Board to report on particulate matter released by tanker ships importing oil into California.
California consumes 1.8 million barrels of oil each day, of which less than 400,000 are produced by in-state workers.
California's demand for oil causes consumers to send more than $25 billion a year to other countries for imported oil.
Petroleum is used to produce more than 6,000 everyday products, including phones and heart valves.
According to the California Independent Petroleum Association, California's oil industry directly supports 55,000 jobs with an average annual pay of $123,000.
The average salary in California is $63,783 per year, approximately half of the average salary in the oil industry.
Senate Bill 15 aims to bring back jobs lost to foreign countries that do not respect environmental or democratic values.
